title:
PART 3: meal prep for fat loss! 

🌮 Breakfast Tacos (serves 1)

Ingredients:

* 2 carbonaut gf tort
description:
PART 3: meal prep for fat loss! 

🌮 Breakfast Tacos (serves 1)

Ingredients:

* 2 carbonaut gf tortillas
* 1 egg 
* 3 egg whites 
* 1/2 cup cottage cheese
* 2 tbsp salsa verde
* Pinch salt + pepper
* Red pepper flakes (optional)

Instructions:

1. Scramble eggs with cottage cheese, salt, and pepper.
2. Warm tortillas.
3. Fill tortillas with eggs.
4. Top with salsa + red pepper flakes.

Macros:
327 cal | 20g protein | 19.5g fat | 32.7g carbs | 26.8g fiber

⸻

🥗 Grilled Chicken Mediterranean Bowl (serves 4)

Ingredients:

* 1 cup chopped parsley
* 2 medium tomatoes, diced
* 5 mini cucumbers, chopped
* 8 green onions, sliced
* 1 tbsp chopped mint
* Juice of 2 lemons
* 2 tbsp olive oil
* ½ tsp salt
* ½ tsp black pepper
* 1 lb chicken breast, sliced
* 8 tbsp Ithaca Hummus

Chicken Marinade:

* 3 tbsp olive oil
* Juice of 2 lemons
* 3 cloves garlic, minced
* ½ tsp salt
* 1 tsp black pepper

Instructions:

1. Mix marinade and coat chicken. Marinate 30 minutes.
2. Chop veggies + herbs.
3. Toss with lemon juice, olive oil, salt, and pepper.
4. Grill chicken 4–5 minutes per side.
5. Build bowls with tabouli, sliced chicken, and hummus.

Macros (per serving):
377 cal | 29g protein | 22g fat | 17g net carbs | 4.5g fiber

⸻

🍎 Apple + Almond Butter (serves 1)

Ingredients:

* 1 medium Envy apple
* 1 tbsp sugar-free almond butter

Instructions:

1. Slice apple.
2. Dip in almond butter.

Macros:
180 cal | 4g protein | 8g fat | 22g net carbs | 4g fiber

⸻

🍯 Hot Honey Beef Bowl (serves 1)

Ingredients:

* 5 oz 93% lean ground beef
* ⅔ cup sweet potato croutons
* ½ cup 4% cottage cheese
* 1.5 oz avocado
* 1 tsp hot honey
* ½ tsp steak seasoning

Instructions:

1. Sauté ground beef with steak seasoning over medium heat for 6–8 minutes, breaking it up, until browned.
2. Bake sweet potatoes at 400°F for ~25 minutes, flipping halfway.
3. Slice avocado.
4. Plate beef, sweet potatoes, cottage cheese, and avocado.
5. Drizzle hot honey on top.

Macros:
541 cal | 45.6g protein | 25.7g fat | 23.8g net carbs | 6.8g fiber

title:
🌈 EAT THE RAINBOW! 🌈
Tired of boring salads? This Rainbow Chicken Salad is a feast for your eyes a
description:
🌈 EAT THE RAINBOW! 🌈
Tired of boring salads? This Rainbow Chicken Salad is a feast for your eyes and your taste buds! Packed with vibrant veggies and topped with a creamy, nutty Almond Honey Mustard Dressing, it’s the ultimate healthy lunch that actually keeps you full. 🥗✨
Ingredients:
The Protein: 2 grilled chicken breasts (sliced)
The Colors: Chopped romaine, shredded purple cabbage, yellow bell peppers, cherry tomatoes, and sliced cucumbers.
The Crunch: Sliced almonds and a handful of dried cranberries or pomegranate seeds.
The Almond Honey Mustard Dressing: 3 tbsp almond butter, 2 tbsp Dijon mustard, 2 tbsp honey, 1 tbsp apple cider vinegar, and a pinch of salt. (Add water to reach your desired consistency!)
Instructions:
Chop: Get all your veggies ready. The finer the chop, the better the crunch in every bite!
Whisk: In a small bowl, whisk the almond butter, mustard, honey, and vinegar until smooth. Slowly stir in water until it's creamy and pourable.
Layer: In a large bowl, start with the romaine and layer your "rainbow" of veggies on top.
Top: Add the sliced grilled chicken and a generous sprinkle of crunchy almonds.
Drizzle: Pour that liquid gold dressing over everything and toss gently. 🍯🥢
Pro-Tip: This dressing doubles as a fantastic dip for apple slices or celery! 🍎
Tag your meal-prep partner! 👇
#RainbowSalad #HealthyLunch #HoneyMustard #CleanEating #SaladGoals #MealPrepIdeas #EatBetter

title:
Brain fog by 10am? Look at your breakfast

Most busy parents think breakfast is just about energy.
description:
Brain fog by 10am? Look at your breakfast

Most busy parents think breakfast is just about energy.

But it’s also about how you show up for your day.

When you wake up, your body naturally ramps up alertness (that morning cortisol spike).

What you eat in that window?

It matters more than you think.

Because protein isn’t just for muscle…

It fuels your brain.

It provides the building blocks for:

• dopamine (focus and motivation)
• norepinephrine (alertness)
• serotonin (mood)

Now compare that to the typical busy-parent breakfast:

Toast. Cereal. Grab-and-go carbs.

Quick spike… then a crash.

And that crash shows up as:

• brain fog by mid-morning
• low energy by school drop-off
• poor focus at work
• irritability with the kids

Sound familiar?

Protein changes that.

🔥It steadies your blood sugar.
🔥It keeps your energy consistent.
🔥It helps you think clearer and respond better, not just react.

And for a busy parent…

That matters.

Because it’s not just about getting through the morning.

It’s about how you lead your day.

Coffee can help…

But it won’t fix a poor breakfast.

Start with protein.

And watch how your mornings (and your patience) improve.

If you’ve been running on coffee and carbs…

This might be the simplest upgrade you make this week.

Dm me PROTEIN for the details

description:
Your first bite in the morning can set the tone for your whole day.

Want a healthier breakfast? Keep this simple formula in mind:
Healthy breakfast = protein + fiber + healthy fats + some carbs

Examples:
▪️Protein: eggs, unsweetened yogurt, Greek yogurt, tofu, cottage cheese
▪️Fiber: fruit, oats, whole wheat bread, veggies
▪️Healthy fats: nuts, avocado, peanut butter, chia seeds
▪️Carbs: oats, whole wheat toast, sweet potato, banana

Why it helps:
✅Keeps you full longer
✅Helps prevent mid-morning snack cravings
✅Supports steady energy and better focus
✅Can make weight control easier

title:
vegan blueberry vanilla overnight oats 🫐🤍

follow 👉🏻 @healthyhelper and SAVE this recipe to make
description:
vegan blueberry vanilla overnight oats 🫐🤍

follow 👉🏻 @healthyhelper and SAVE this recipe to make at home!

my GO-TO vegan overnight oats recipe for a quick + protein-packed breakfast you can make ahead.

makes 2 servings! 🤩

ingredients ↓
-1 cup @bobsredmill rolled oats
-1 tbsp chia seeds
-1 ½ cups unsweetened almond milk
-2 scoops @ora.organic vanilla protein (dc: healthyhelper)
-½ cup fresh or frozen @blueberries
-2 tbsp @barneybutter almond butter
-1 tsp @thedatelady date syrup (dc: healthyhelper)
-pinch of salt

optional toppings:

-more almond butter (duh!!)
-fresh blueberries
-chopped almonds

how to make it ↓
-add all ingredients to a blender and blend until mostly smooth. pour into 2 jars or containers, cover, and refrigerate overnight (or at least 4 hours).
-in the morning, give it a stir and top with your favorites. enjoy cold!

packed with protein, fiber, and healthy fats to keep you satisfied until lunch! 💪🏻

title: Protein is encouraged in the morning to break-fast with because it can help support more stable bloo
description:
Protein is encouraged in the morning to break-fast with because it can help support more stable blood sugar, steadier energy, and better satiety throughout the day. This can be extra valuable for women going through perimenopause and menopause because midlife hormone shifts can make blood sugar swings feel more noticeable.

Starting the day with coffee alone may leave some women feeling jittery, hungry, or reaching for carbs by mid-morning.

A protein-forward breakfast can help support:
• A more gradual blood sugar response
• Fewer cravings later in the day
• Better fullness and appetite regulation
• Muscle maintenance (which becomes increasingly important as we age)
• More sustained energy and focus

Aim for 25–30 grams of protein at breakfast.
Here are some ideas 💡
• Eggs + avocado
• Greek yogurt with nuts and seeds
• Cottage cheese bowl
• Protein smoothie
• Leftover salmon or chicken for a savory breakfast

And yes—you can absolutely still have your coffee. I’m simply suggesting don’t let coffee BE breakfast. Pair it with protein, or have your coffee alongside or after a meal. ☕️ 

#MenopauseHealth #PerimenopauseSupport #ProteinBreakfast #BloodSugarBalance #AskDrTami

title:
Day 67 of 365 - Benji 🖤

Some of the cats we care for, we’ve known for over 13 years… isn’t that ju
description:
Day 67 of 365 - Benji 🖤

Some of the cats we care for, we’ve known for over 13 years… isn’t that just incredible?

A huge part of what we do is forming beautiful, lasting bonds with the cats in our care — your cats 🖤

Some love to play, some adore a cuddle, and some prefer us simply to be nearby (but not too close!). We always let them choose, because every cat is an individual — and each visit is tailored to what’s best for them.

For some, we’re there for their entire lives… creating calm, comforting, familiar visits along the way.

What a privilege that is. 🤍 

#catsofedinburgh #catsofinstagram #thefelinecarecompany #catsitteredinburgh

title:
Spicy Shrimp & Bacon Stuffed Jalapeños 🌶️🍤🥓
Bold, creamy, and loaded with flavor! These jalapeños
description:
Spicy Shrimp & Bacon Stuffed Jalapeños 🌶️🍤🥓
Bold, creamy, and loaded with flavor! These jalapeños are stuffed with a rich cheesy filling, topped with Cajun-spiced shrimp and crispy bacon—perfect for an irresistible appetizer.
Ingredients 🛒
10–12 jalapeños, halved & deseeded
200g shrimp, peeled & deveined
6 slices bacon, cooked & crumbled
200g cream cheese, softened
½ cup shredded cheddar cheese
2 tbsp mayonnaise
2 cloves garlic, minced
1 tsp Cajun seasoning
½ tsp smoked paprika
Salt & black pepper to taste
1 tbsp olive oil
Fresh parsley, chopped
Directions 👩‍🍳
1️⃣ Preheat oven to 190°C (375°F). Line a baking tray.
2️⃣ In a bowl, mix cream cheese, cheddar, mayo, garlic, paprika, and a pinch of Cajun seasoning until smooth.
3️⃣ Fill each jalapeño half with the creamy mixture.
4️⃣ Toss shrimp with olive oil, Cajun seasoning, salt, and pepper.
5️⃣ Sear shrimp in a hot pan for 1–2 minutes per side until just cooked.
6️⃣ Place one shrimp on top of each stuffed jalapeño and sprinkle with crumbled bacon.
7️⃣ Bake for 12–15 minutes until bubbly and slightly golden.
8️⃣ Garnish with fresh parsley and serve warm.
⏰ Prep Time: 15 minutes
⏰ Cook Time: 15 minutes
🍽 Servings: 4
🔥 Calories: ~320 kcal per serving
#appetizers #spicyfood #easyrecipes #partyfood #seafood

title:
Soft & Chewy Vegan Lemon Brownies with Lemon Glaze

Bright, zesty, and perfectly chewy, these lemon
description:
Soft & Chewy Vegan Lemon Brownies with Lemon Glaze

Bright, zesty, and perfectly chewy, these lemon brownies deliver bold citrus flavor without any dairy or eggs. Plus, that glossy glaze seals the deal.

Servings: 12

Ingredients:

1 ½ cups all-purpose flour
1 cup sugar
½ tsp baking powder
¼ tsp salt
½ cup plant-based milk
⅓ cup vegetable oil
2 tbsp lemon zest
¼ cup fresh lemon juice
1 tsp vanilla extract

For the Lemon Glaze:

1 cup powdered sugar
2–3 tbsp lemon juice

Instructions:
Preheat oven to 180°C. Line a square pan.
Mix flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt.
In another bowl, whisk milk, oil, lemon zest, juice, and vanilla.
Combine wet and dry ingredients until smooth.
Pour batter into the pan evenly.
Bake for 20–25 minutes until set.
Cool completely before glazing.
Whisk powdered sugar with lemon juice until smooth.
Spread glaze over the brownies.
Let it set, then slice into squares.

Would you add extra zest on top or keep the glaze smooth?

title:
High-protein chicken Alfredo bake 🤍
This is SO creamy and decadent you’d never guess it’s a lighter
description:
High-protein chicken Alfredo bake 🤍
This is SO creamy and decadent you’d never guess it’s a lighter swap… and it comes together in no time! .

High-Protein Chicken Alfredo Bake

Ingredients (Serves ~6)

* 2-3 cups steamed broccoli (bite-size, cooked until al dente)
* ~20 oz cooked rotisserie chicken breast, shredded or chopped
* 160g Trader Joe’s Lite Shredded Mozzarella (about 1 ⅓ cups), divided
* 2.5 oz grated Parmesan cheese, divided

Sauce:

* 1 (15 oz) jar Rao’s Alfredo Sauce
* 1 cup Fairlife Reduced Fat Milk
* 1 cup (8 oz) Good Culture Low-Fat Cottage Cheese
* 1 tsp garlic powder
* ½ tsp onion powder
* ½ tsp salt
* ½ tsp black pepper

⸻

Instructions

1. Preheat oven to 375°F.
2. Add steamed broccoli to the bottom of a 10x7 casserole dish.
3. Sprinkle half of the mozzarella and Parmesan over the broccoli.
4. Layer the cooked chicken evenly on top.
5. In a bowl, mix together the Alfredo sauce, milk, cottage cheese, and seasonings until smooth and well combined.
6. Pour the sauce evenly over the casserole, gently poking around so it settles throughout (no need to fully mix).
7. Top with the remaining mozzarella and Parmesan.
8. Bake uncovered for 35 minutes, or until hot and bubbly.
9. Broil for 2–3 minutes until the top is golden and slightly crispy.

⸻

Calories & Macros (per serving, ~6 servings)

* Calories: ~385
* Protein: ~47g
* Carbs: ~8–10g
* Fat: ~18g

(Will vary slightly depending on exact chicken + cheese brands)

Honey soy chicken 🍗 
Ingredients: 
500g chicken thigh

Bokchoy or vegetables of choice 

Honey soy marinade for chicken: 
1/2 cup soy sauce 
3/4 cup honey 
1/3 cup water 
1/4 cup Chinese cooking wine 
1 tbsp sesame oil 
2 cloves garlic 

Serving sauce marinade: 
1/3 cup water 
1 1/2 tsp corn flour 

Method: 
Honey soy marinade: Mix the ingredients together, set aside 1/2 cup to use for the serving sauce. Pour the remainder of the marinade on the raw chicken and let it marinate.
Serving sauce: Mix the 1/2 cup of serving sauce with water and corn flour. Simmer over medium heat until it thickens. 
Chicken: cook the chicken. Serve with greens, sesame seeds and pour the serving sauce on top.

description:
High Protein Big Mac 🔥 recipe below

1. Finely dice 1/2 a brown onion and 3 pickles. Mix 1/2 cup fat free mayonnaise with diced pickles, 1 tbsp diced onion, 1 tsp paprika, 1 tsp onion powder, 1 tsp American mustard, 1 tbsp tomato sauce and 1 tsp white vinegar for the sauce. Season with salt if needed. 
2. Slice 1 cup of iceberg lettuce very finely and set aside 
3. Cut the bottom crust off of one burger bun to create the middle bun and toast in a dry, hot cast iron pan/bbq 
4. Make 2, 125g balls of extra lean beef mince and place them onto a super hot cast iron pan/bbq. Smash down with a burger smasher or flat surface and season with salt and pepper. Flip once a nice crust has formed on the bottom side and add some more diced onion, followed by a slice of American cheese on each pattie. 
5. Build your burger by adding sauce to the bottom bun, followed by lettuce, pattie, pickles, middle bun, more sauce, lettuce, pattie and finally top bun with more sauce.

description:
Easy Outfit Change Transition | InShot Editing Tutorial 📱
————

Here’s an easy transition you can try for your next Get Ready With Me, Try-On Haul, or Outfit Of The Day video.

I just want to add, if you plan on using tape, I’d suggest using clear tape and putting down a really small piece so it can’t be seen. The tape I used in the video was the first thing I found, so don’t mind that lol.

The key to making this transition successful is making sure that you stand relatively in the same spot. Make sure you don’t move your camera and try to maintain consistent lighting if possible. 

I hope you enjoyed this editing tutorial! 💬 Let me know if you’ll be trying this out! #contentcreatortips #easyeditingtutorial #easytransitionideas #inshoteditingtutorials

Here’s everything I did in Tokyo for under $40 USD ‼️

I booked the ‘Have fun in Tokyo Pass’ on Klook that gave me access to things like a yakitori lunch in the heart of Shinjuku and a public bath facility RIGHT in front of Tokyo Dome. 

With the pass, you just show your QR code to the staff at each location and don’t have to worry about paying or figuring out the prices. 

The day I was at the spa, was actually when Aespa was performing at Tokyo Dome & I literally heard the performance happening as I was in the outdoor bath area so that’s a double win I guess 😂 

Anyways make sure to use code: WHATTHEPATO10 when booking through Klook for some extra $$ off :)

description:
Shibuya Excel Tokyu
📍 1 Chome-12-2 Dogenzaka, Shibuya, Tokyo 150-0043, Japan

USD$1337 total for the “King Bed” Room PARK VIEW for 3 nights for 2 adults
 
‪Pros:‬
🟢 has its own limousine bus stop to both HND/NRT Airports accessed  on the floor of the lobby —this was PERFECT since it was our last hotel before we had to go home! 
🟢 location was literally the best in all of Shibuya—I will die on this hill
🟢 Shibuya Sky and Shibuya station connected to the hotel by a sky walk
🟢 under the hotel is literally Mark City 
🟢 came with lots of in room supplies 
🟢 hotel room had black out curtains & such an unbeatable view of Tokyo
🟢 had the most in room outlets out of every hotel we went too
🟢 had beer vending machines on each floor 
🟢 they do luggage forward and receiving
🟢 fluent English speaking staff
🟢 very clean and quiet hotel 
🟢 the location and accessiblility was worth the price 
🟢 I will 100% always return to this hotel whenever I visit Japan

Cons:
🟠 pricey depending on seasons and the room we got wasn’t the most updated in comparison to all the other hotels we stayed at but it was very well maintained and clean
🟠 finding the entrance is hard the first time if you didn’t do much looking on Google Maps ahead of time

I Turned Ground Beef Into THIS… and Now I Make It Every Week 🔥

description:
I Turned Ground Beef Into THIS… and Now I Make It Every Week 🔥

I discovered a new way to prepare ground beef, and it was so good that it’s already become a permanent part of our weekly dinner rotation! If you’re tired of the same old recipes, you have to see how I transform basic ingredients into something truly extraordinary.
This dish is the definition of comfort food, rich, savory, and incredibly satisfying. Whether you're cooking for a busy family or just want a restaurant-quality meal without the hassle, this recipe is for you. It’s quick enough for a weeknight but special enough for a weekend treat.

In this video, you’ll discover:
• The secret to getting the most flavor out of your ground beef.
• A simple technique for a velvety, mouth-watering sauce.
• How to create a "new favorite" meal using pantry staples.

I love sharing these simple kitchen wins with you all. It’s proof that you don't need to be a professional chef to make something that tastes absolutely amazing!

Ingredients:
1 lb (450g) ground beef
1 tbsp butter
1 shallot
1 carrot
2 garlic cloves
½ tsp garlic powder
½ tsp black pepper
Few fresh thyme sprigs or ½ tsp dried
1 tsp salt
½ tsp black pepper
1 ½ tbsp sherry vinegar
2 heaping tablespoons of flour
1 tbsp Worcestershire sauce
3/4 cup heavy cream
3 cups hot water or beef stock
1 cup frozen peas
½ cup Parmesan cheese
1 pack store bought puff pastry
1 egg (optional)
Parsley

Raising Cane’s at Home 🐔🏠

For ALL of you asking - the app link for Osta is in my bio or you can s
description:
Raising Cane’s at Home 🐔🏠

For ALL of you asking - the app link for Osta is in my bio or you can search “Osta Recipes” on the App Store or Google Play store ‼️🫶

Crispy buttermilk chicken tenders, addictive Cane’s sauce and fluffy texas toast… all at home! 

For the Chicken:
-2 lbs (0.9 kg) chicken breast, sliced into strips (or use tenderloins)
-2 cups buttermilk
-1/3 cup pickle juice
-Seasonings: 1 tsp garlic powder, 1 tsp paprika, 1 tsp onion powder, 1 tsp salt, 1 tsp black pepper
-Neutral oil, for frying
Flour Mixture:
-2 cups all-purpose flour +1/2 cup cornstarch
-1 tsp baking powder
-Seasonings: 2 tsp garlic powder, 2 tsp paprika, 2 tsp salt, 2 tsp black pepper
Cane’s Sauce
-1/2 cup mayonnaise
-1/4 cup ketchup
-2 tsp Worcestershire sauce
-Seasonings: 1 tsp garlic powder, 1 tsp onion powder, 1 tsp paprika, 1 tsp black pepper, 1 tsp seasoning salt

Instructions:
1. Slice the chicken breasts into strips about 1 inch thick and 3–4 inches long (roughly tender-sized).
In a bowl, whisk together the buttermilk, pickle juice, and chicken seasonings until fully combined.
Add the chicken and toss to coat. Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours, or overnight for extra tender chicken.
2. In a large bowl, combine the flour, cornstarch, baking powder, and flour mixture seasonings. Mix.
3. Add neutral oil to a large pot or deep skillet until it is about 2–3 inches deep. Heat the oil to 350°F / 175°C.  Keeping the oil around this temperature ensures crispy chicken without burning the coating.
4. Drizzle a little buttermilk marinade into the flour mixture and stir to create flaky, crispy bits. Shake excess marinade off the chicken, then coat each piece thoroughly in the flour mixture. Shake off any extra flour and gently place into the hot oil.
5. Don’t touch them for the first 1–2 mins, that’s how you get the perfect crust. Fry for 7–10 mins, or until golden brown and cooked through. Transfer the chicken to a paper towel-lined rack to drain.
6. In a bowl, combine all the sauce ingredients then refrigerate. 
7. Serve the chicken with a side of sauce, texas toast & crinkle fries… Enjoy :)

title:
Beef Bourguignon

Serves
4–6

Ingredients

1kg x Beef Brisket, chuck, or another slow-cooking cut
Sa
description:
Beef Bourguignon

Serves
4–6

Ingredients

1kg x Beef Brisket, chuck, or another slow-cooking cut
Salt x and Black Pepper
30ml x Olive Oil
20g x Butter
300g x Bacon Lardons
2 x Medium Onions, thinly sliced
4 x Medium Carrots, peeled and cut on the diagonal
2 x Cloves Garlic, crushed
40-45g x Tomato Paste (tomato purée)
30g x Plain Flour
750ml x Red Wine (1 bottle)
300ml x Beef Stock
A few sprigs fresh Thyme
2 x Bay Leaves

Finishing
6 x Shallots, peeled and halved
200g x Button mushrooms
Olive Oil
20g x Butter
Salt and Black Pepper, to taste
Fresh Parsley, chopped

Method

Prepare the Beef
Cut the beef into 4–5 cm chunks. Season generously with salt and black pepper, tossing to coat all sides.
Set aside.

Cook the Bacon
Place a large heavy pot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the olive oil and then the bacon lardons.
Cook until the fat renders out and the bacon becomes golden and crisp.
Remove from the pot and set aside.

Sear the Beef
In the same pot, add the beef in batches if needed.
Sear until deeply browned on all sides.
Partway through cooking, add 20 g butter to help develop colour and flavour.
Remove the beef and set aside.

Cook the Vegetables
Add the onions, carrots, and garlic to the same pot.
Sauté until the onions start to take on a little colour.
Stir in the tomato paste and cook for 1–2 minutes.
Add the flour, stir to coat the vegetables, and cook for another 1–2 minutes.

Build the Stew
Return the beef and bacon to the pot.
Pour in: 750ml red wine, 300ml beef stock
Add the thyme and bay leaves.
Bring to a gentle simmer.

Slow Cook
Cover with a lid and cook:
Stovetop
Simmer gently for 1½–2 hours, stirring occasionally.
OR Oven
Cook in a 160°C fan oven for 2–3 hours.
The beef should become very tender.
If the sauce reduces too much during cooking, add a splash of water.

Cook the Mushrooms and Shallots
Near the end of cooking:
Heat a frying pan over medium-high heat.
Add a drizzle of olive oil and 20g butter.
Cook the mushrooms until browned, then remove.
Add the shallots cut side down and cook until nicely caramelised. Flip and cook briefly.
Remove from the pan.

Finish the Dish
Add the mushrooms and shallots to the stew.
Simmer for a final 20 minutes.
Season to taste with salt and black pepper.
Finish with fresh chopped parsley.

Serve
Serve hot with:
* Mashed potatoes
* Crusty bread
* Buttered pasta
* Or pommes purée
It’s even better the next day.

title: Hassle back potatoes with creamy Cajun chicken and airfried brocollini . Easy, healthy comfort meal
description:
Hassle back potatoes with creamy Cajun chicken and airfried brocollini . Easy, healthy comfort meal 

Potato recipe
6 medium potatoes
3 tbsp butter (melted)
2 tbsp olive oil
4–5 cloves garlic (minced)
1½ tsp paprika
1½ tsp onion granules
1½ tsp garlic granules
1 tsp dried parsley
Salt & pepper
1–2 tsp all-purpose seasoning
Method:
Slice each potato thinly (don’t cut all the way through).
Mix melted butter, olive oil, garlic, and all seasonings.
Brush generously over all 6 potatoes, getting into the layers.
Air fry at 180°C for 35–45 mins (depending on size).
Brush with extra butter halfway through for that crispy finish.

Chicken recipe 
Ingredients:
1 tbsp olive oil
1½ tsp Cajun seasoning
½ tsp salt
½ tsp black pepper
1 tsp paprika
1 tsp onion powder
Method:
Add the sliced chicken to a bowl.
Drizzle over the olive oil.
Sprinkle all the seasonings evenly.
Mix well until every piece is fully coated.
Let it sit for 10–15 minutes (optional, but boosts flavor).
Pan-fry on medium-high heat until golden and cooked through.

Cajun Sauce:
1 tbsp butter
½ onion (diced)
150–200ml heavy cream
Same seasonings as chicken
Instructions 
In the same pan, melt butter and cook onions until soft.
Add cream + seasoning, simmer until thickened.
Add chicken back in and coat in sauce.
